title: Implement TypeScript conversations, backpressure, connection, and codegen
purpose: Complete the TypeScript SDK with Conversation<T> lifecycle management (ADR-001), backpressure as discriminated unions (ADR-004), connection lifecycle and reconnection, and a type generator that produces TypeScript interfaces from agora channel definitions. After this brief, TypeScript developers get IDE autocompletion and type checking for channel messages without writing type definitions manually (story S8).
task: Create conversation.ts, pressure.ts, connection.ts, and codegen.ts in sdks/agora-ts/src/. Conversation<T> mirrors the Rust ConversationHandle with lifecycle events as a TypeScript-idiomatic interface. PressureResponse is a discriminated union matching the Rust enum. The connection module implements the same state machine as SDK-003 (connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected) with exponential backoff. The codegen module reads agora channel type definitions and generates TypeScript interfaces. IN SCOPE: conversations, backpressure unions, connection lifecycle, type generation. OUT OF SCOPE: channel logic (SDK-007), WASM bridge changes (SDK-007).

requirements.title: Define Conversation<T> interface with lifecycle events
requirements.spec: WHEN a TypeScript developer opens a Conversation<T>, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a handle with send, receive, and close methods. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit lifecycle events (opened, message, closing, closed, error) as an observable stream. WHEN the conversation errors, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit an error event with a typed reason before transitioning to closed. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT silently drop lifecycle transitions.
acceptance: Conversation<T> interface has send(message: T): Promise<void> method
Conversation<T> interface has receive(): AsyncIterable<T> method
Conversation<T> interface has close(): Promise<void> method
Conversation<T>.lifecycle returns an AsyncIterable<ConversationEvent> or EventEmitter
ConversationEvent is a discriminated union with kind: 'opened' | 'message' | 'closing' | 'closed' | 'error'
Error events carry a typed reason field

requirements.title: Define backpressure discriminated unions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a PressureResponse discriminated union with three variants: { kind: 'accept' }, { kind: 'defer', delay: number } (delay in milliseconds), and { kind: 'reject', reason: string }. WHEN Channel.publish returns a PressureResponse, THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve the variant without flattening or converting. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT silently convert 'defer' or 'reject' to 'accept'.
acceptance: PressureResponse is a discriminated union type with kind as the discriminant
Accept variant: { kind: 'accept' }
Defer variant: { kind: 'defer', delay: number } where delay is in milliseconds
Reject variant: { kind: 'reject', reason: string }
Channel.publish return type is Promise<PressureResponse>
TypeScript narrowing works: if (response.kind === 'defer') { response.delay } compiles

requirements.title: Implement connection lifecycle and reconnection
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK connects to an agora server, THE SYSTEM SHALL manage the connection through states: connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected. WHEN the connection drops, THE SYSTEM SHALL automatically reconnect using exponential backoff with jitter. WHEN reconnection succeeds, THE SYSTEM SHALL recover subscriptions from their last acknowledged sequence. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit connection state change events for observability. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry.
acceptance: ConnectionState is a discriminated union: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'reconnecting' | 'disconnected'
Connection emits state change events observable via onStateChange callback or EventEmitter
Reconnection delay follows exponential backoff: min(baseDelay * 2^attempt, maxDelay) + jitter
After reconnection, subscriptions resume from their last acknowledged sequence
No fixed-interval retry code path exists
baseDelay and maxDelay are configurable via ConnectionConfig

requirements.title: Implement type generator from channel definitions
requirements.spec: WHEN a developer runs the type generator against agora channel type definitions, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce TypeScript interface files matching each channel's message schema. THE SYSTEM SHALL generate interfaces with proper field names, types, and JSDoc comments. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require manual type definition for channels that have agora-registered schemas.
acceptance: codegen.ts exports a generate function that accepts channel type definitions and outputs TypeScript source
Generated interfaces match channel message schemas with correct field names and TypeScript types
Generated files include JSDoc comments with the source channel name and schema version
A channel with fields { name: string, count: integer } produces interface { name: string; count: number; }
The generator is usable as a CLI tool or programmatic API

boundaries: SHALL NOT duplicate channel publish/subscribe logic from SDK-007.
SHALL NOT modify the WASM bridge from SDK-007.
SHALL NOT silently consume backpressure signals.
SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry for reconnection.
verification: TypeScript compilation (tsc --noEmit) succeeds with no errors
Conversation lifecycle events emit in correct order: opened -> message* -> closing -> closed
PressureResponse discriminated union narrows correctly in TypeScript
Connection reconnection uses exponential backoff verified by unit test
Type generator produces valid TypeScript from a test channel definition
Generated TypeScript interfaces compile without errors
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
